claim_text: T1 of 297 ms for 2 H-Lac and 320 ms for HDO at 11.7 T)37.

sub_claims.text: De Feyter et al. 2018 reports T1 = 297 ms for 2H-Lac (deuterated lactate) at 11.7 T.

evidence.snippet: The T1 and T2 values of deuterated water and glucose were measured in water and agar phantoms and in rat and human brain in vivo. In addition, the relaxation parameters for Glx were measured in rat and human brain in vivo, whereas deuterated lactate T1, T2, and T2* values were measured in rat brain postmortem (tables S1 and S2).
The T1 values of [6,6′-2H2]glucose and its downstream metabolites range from 60 to 350 ms.
Animal studies were performed on an 11.7 T Magnex magnet (Magnex Scientific Ltd.) interfaced to a Bruker Avance III HD spectrometer running on ParaVision 6 (Bruker Instruments).

Signal variations caused by incomplete T1 relaxation and RF transmit B1+ magnetic field inhomogeneity were corrected using T1 relaxation time constants measured in vivo (table S1) and quantitative B1+ maps acquired on a phantom containing water, 1.5% agar, and 1% deuteriumoxide.

sub_claims.paper_value: Deuterated lactate T1, T2, T2* measured in rat brain postmortem at 11.7 T (tables S1 and S2; specific numerical value not present in ingested main text); T1 range 60-350 ms for glucose and downstream metabolites, which is consistent with 297 ms.
sub_claims.nuance: Defeyter2018 does measure 2H-Lac T1 at 11.7 T (rat brain postmortem) and reports the specific values in supplementary table S1/S2 (referenced in content.txt L4 and L45). The ingested handle does not include the supplementary table, so the specific value 297 ms cannot be verified directly against the ingested main text, but the range 60-350 ms for 2H-glucose/downstream metabolites at 11.7 T is consistent with 297 ms. Flag NEEDS_SUPPLEMENT to close the quantitative check.

sub_claims.text: De Feyter et al. 2018 reports T1 = 320 ms for HDO (deuterated water) at 11.7 T.

evidence.snippet: The T1 and T2 values of deuterated water and glucose were measured in water and agar phantoms and in rat and human brain in vivo... (tables S1 and S2).
Animal studies were performed on an 11.7 T Magnex magnet (Magnex Scientific Ltd.).

T1, T2, and T2* measurements T1 values of water, glucose, Glx, and lactate were estimated on the basis of a threeparameter fit of the signal amplitude acquired using a nonlocalized inversion-recovery method with 12 logarithmically spaced inversion times between 10 and 2560 ms and a TR of 2560 ms.

sub_claims.paper_value: Deuterated water (HDO) T1 measured in phantoms and rat/human brain in vivo at 11.7 T; specific numerical value reported in supplementary table S1/S2 (not ingested).
sub_claims.claim_value: T1 = 320 ms for HDO at 11.7 T
sub_claims.nuance: Defeyter2018 clearly measures HDO T1 at 11.7 T via inversion recovery (content.txt L4, L42) and tabulates the value in Supplementary Table S1. The specific number 320 ms cannot be verified against the ingested main-text content; it is consistent with published 2H water T1 at 11.7 T in rat brain. NEEDS_SUPPLEMENT to confirm the exact value.

remediation.suggested_edit: Where these specific T1 values are cited (e.g., 'T1 of 297 ms for 2H-Lac and 320 ms for HDO at 11.7 T'), point reviewers to the specific Supplementary Table S1 in De Feyter et al. 2018 (e.g., 'Table S1 of De Feyter et al. 2018'). Alternatively, re-verify against the supplement to ensure the numbers are transcribed correctly; defeyter2018 measures lactate T1 postmortem in rat brain at 11.7 T and water T1 in phantoms and in vivo at 11.7 T, and both sit within the 60-350 ms range reported in the main text.

phrasings_tried: deuterated lactate T1 postmortem inversion recovery

attestation.closest_adjacent: content.txt L4: '...deuterated lactate T1, T2, and T2* values were measured in rat brain postmortem (tables S1 and S2).' Plus L5: 'The T1 values of [6,6′-2H2]glucose and its downstream metabolites range from 60 to 350 ms.' These confirm defeyter2018 is a plausible source for 2H-Lac T1 (in the 60-350 ms range) and for HDO T1 at 11.7 T, but the specific 297 ms / 320 ms numbers live in the supplement.
attestation.indirect_attribution_check: Defeyter2018's 2H relaxation parameters are primary measurements by the authors; no indirect attribution concern.
attestation.out_of_context_check: Context matches: the manuscript uses these T1 numbers as characteristic 11.7 T values for 2H-Lac and HDO, and defeyter2018 measures exactly those parameters at 11.7 T. The only gap is that the specific numeric values are in supplementary tables not included in the ingested handle.
